CLINICAL NOTES — OPHTHALMOLOGY
Patient: Martinez, Carlos | DOB: 08/22/1962 | MRN: MRN34567890
Date of Service: 11/15/2025
Provider: Dr. Jane Smith, MD — Eye Care Center

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Follow-up for exudative (wet) age-related macular degeneration (AMD), bilateral.

DIAGNOSIS: H35.32 — Exudative age-related macular degeneration, bilateral

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Martinez is a 62-year-old male with a 3-year history of exudative AMD. He has been receiving intravitreal anti-VEGF injections (ranibizumab) on a treat-and-extend protocol. His last injection was 6 weeks ago. He reports worsening of central vision in the right eye over the past 2 weeks with new metamorphopsia.

OCULAR EXAMINATION:
- Visual Acuity: OD 20/80 (decreased from 20/50 at last visit), OS 20/40
- IOP: OD 16 mmHg, OS 15 mmHg
- Anterior Segment: Pseudophakia OU, otherwise unremarkable
- Dilated Fundus Exam: OD — subretinal fluid with pigment epithelial detachment (PED) in the macula; OS — dry macula with drusen

OCT FINDINGS:
- OD: Central subfield thickness 387 microns (increased from 298 microns). Subretinal fluid present with PED. Active choroidal neovascularization (CNV) membrane.
- OS: Central subfield thickness 268 microns, stable. No subretinal fluid.

TREATMENT HI

args.text: Triage review for DEN-001 / CLM-2025-00001: Aetna PPO denied both lines as CO-50 medical necessity (remarks N657/N386) for DOS 11/15/2025. Claim includes CPT 67028 intravitreal injection and J2778 ranibizumab for primary dx H35.32 exudative age-related macular degeneration, bilateral; remaining balance $2,450. Clinical note from Dr. Jane Smith supports medical necessity: established wet AMD on treat-and-extend anti-VEGF therapy, worsening central vision/metamorphopsia, VA OD declined from 20/50 to 20/80, OCT OD showed increased central thickness 387 microns, subretinal fluid, PED, and active CNV. Denial does not appear to be timely filing, eligibility, prior auth, or coding issue. Appropriate disposition is Route to Clinical Appeals for medical-necessity appeal using the clinical documentation before the 2026-06-01 deadline.
